summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard tape of the legendary 5/26/72 London show – a monster first set and an epic second‑set jam marathon highlighted by Truckin’ > The Other One, Morning Dew and Playing in the Band.
review: This SBD recording captures one of Europe ’72’s apex performances. The band is on fire from the opening Cumberland Blues through the 19‑song first set, then launches into a second‑set suite that strings together Truckin’ > The Other One (three times), a massive drum break and a soaring Morning Dew before closing with Sing Me Back Home. The mix is remarkably clean – crisp guitars, punchy drums and well‑balanced keyboards – making the musical fireworks shine even brighter.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 60 reviews (mostly 5‑star) praise both the performance and the sound quality; only a handful of minor criticisms appear, so the combined show (70 %) + recording (30 %) score is near‑perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: The tape is praised as crisp, clear and well balanced. Reviewers note a tiny hiss that can be cleaned with modest EQ, but overall the mix delivers strong stereo imaging, defined drums, prominent bass and clean keyboards.

standout_songs: China Cat > I Know You Rider
Not Fade Away > G.D. Trunk F***ing Return Back > Not Fade Away
poor_songs: He's Gone (a little lumpy for one reviewer)
show_quality.setlist_flow: A 19‑song first set that feels like a second set in its intensity, highlighted by the NFA > GDTRFB > NFA transition. The second set is an uninterrupted jam suite: Truckin' → The Other One (×3) → Drum solo → Morning Dew → The Other One → Sing Me Back Home, delivering seamless segues and sustained momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for his soaring guitar tones, emotive vocals (especially on Morning Dew), and fearless improvisations that drive the jams.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass lines described as “esoteric” and prominently featured; vocal contributions on He's Gone praised, with occasional bass solos noted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for rhythm‑guitar work and fun moments on Rider and Casey Jones; his interplay with Jerry highlighted during solos.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is singled out for front‑line keyboard work – hammering riffs, weaving into jams, and providing a strong organ/piano presence that balances the mix. Pigpen’s organ also receives positive mention.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drum solo described as “great” and the percussion‑driven sections of The Other One praised for their jazz‑fusion feel.
